meaning: n.,鹦鹉,讲究穿着的人,花花公子
examples: It was a wooden bird, the popinjay used at the shooting-matches at Prastoe.
Might Hooper and Seidler have considered making Logue do the "popinjay" speech by Hotspur from Henry IV Part One - the Shakespeare character traditionally played as a stammerer?
Filmmaker Jonathan Nossiter depicted Rolland as a self-important popinjay, contemptuous of any palate other than his own.
